sections.content: SHANGHAI, China
Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA)

The Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA) is an integrated platform involving medical institutions, universities and research institutions, cosmetic, health food, and medical device companies, as well as digital content and media providers. As a strategic bridge and integrated hub for Korea-China skin health industry cooperation, the Association connects markets, research, and policies to support the enhancement of global competitiveness for companies in both countries.
Roles of the Association

content.heading: 01 Global Bridge
items: Connecting local medical institutions, universities, research institutions, and corporate networks in China
Supporting corporate innovation and technological development through linking industry-academia-research-based Korea-China national projects

content.heading: 02 Accurate Information Sharing
items: Prompt and accurate sharing of information on policy and regulatory changes in China's skin and skincare industry
Providing practical market information, including certification standards and market trends

content.heading: 03 R&D Cooperation Support
items: Discovering innovative technologies through cooperation with authoritative expert groups in China
Supporting new product development through joint research projects with hospitals, universities, and research institutions

content.heading: 04 Localized Marketing
items: Supporting brand promotion through connections with major local media outlets in China
Connecting marketing with medical professionals and expert influencers active on major digital platforms

sections.content: ## Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA)
## 2026 SHANGHAI, China
### Senior Advisor Academician Cheol-Beom Park

Professor, University of Toronto, Canada; International authority in the field of POLYMER FOAM; Academician, Chinese Academy of Engineering; Academician, European Academy of Sciences; Academician, National Academy of Engineering of Korea and Korean Academy of Science and Technology (KAST); Academician, Royal Society of Canada and Canadian Academy of Engineering; Honorary Member, International Engineering and Technology Institute (IETI); Member, American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS); Member, Engineering Institute of Canada (EICC), etc.
40 patents, over 1000 journal articles, SCOPUS H-INDEX 106, 40,638 citations.
### Advisor Professor Yan-Yun Ma

Professor, Institute of Biomedical Engineering and Technology Innovation, Fudan University; Chairman, Oriental-American Dermatology Research Institute; Director, Skin Research and Transformation Center, Changjiang Institute of Science, Fudan University; Director, Shanghai Skin Health Concept Verification Center; Standing Committee Member, Skin Health Committee, Chinese Association of Medical Education; Coordinator, Functional Cosmetics Collaboration; Secretary and Member, Cosmetics Group, Dermatology Branch, China Association of Chinese Medicine and Western Medicine Integration; Deputy Secretary-General, Functional Cosmetics Branch, China Association of Plastic and Aesthetic Medicine; Secretary-General, Cosmetics Professional Committee, Shanghai Medical Industry Association, etc.
100 SCI-indexed publications, over 10 invention patents.
### Advisor Professor Xin Li

Director, Dermatology and Aesthetic Medicine Center, Shanghai Yueyang Hospital of Integrated Traditional Chinese and Western Medicine; Deputy Director, Dermatology Research Institute, Shanghai Academy of Traditional Chinese Medicine; Vice Chairman, Youth Committee, China Association of Chinese Medicine; Chairman, Skin Health Professional Committee, Shanghai Medical Industry Association; Standing Committee Member and Secretary, Professional Committee of Dermatology and Venereology, China Association of Chinese Medicine and Western Medicine Integration, etc.
Ranked 21st in academic influence in the field of Dermatology in China; Selected as one of the World's Top 2% Scientists. 52 SCI-indexed publications (First or Corresponding Author, cumulative IF > 350); Principal Investigator for 11 research projects, including those funded by the National Natural Science Foundation of China.
### Standing Committee Member Professor Yong-Xian Lai

Chief, Department of Dermatologic Surgery, Shanghai Dermatology Hospital / Tongji University Affiliated Hospital; Director, Hair Diagnosis Center; Deputy Director, Skin Tumor Diagnosis Center; Expert, Ministry of Science and Technology of China; Deputy Head, Hair Disease Group, Professional Committee of Dermatologic Rehabilitation, Chinese Association of Rehabilitation Medicine; Secretary, Hairology Group, Professional Committee of Dermatology and Venereology, China Association of Chinese Medicine and Western Medicine Integration; Member, Hair Medicine and Scalp Health Management Group, Professional Committee of Dermatology, China Association of Non-Public Medical Institutions, etc.
Visiting Scholar, University of Zurich, Switzerland; Second Prize for Science and Technology Progress Award, Shanghai Municipality; Principal Investigator for multiple research projects; Over 10 SCI-indexed publications.
### Jae-Kyo Lee, Research Committee Chairman

Research Fellow, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute / Harvard Medical School; Former President, New England Bioscience Society (NEBS).
Research in skin barrier function, sphingolipids, and ceramides, specifically elucidating the mechanism by which ALKALINE CERAMIDASE 1 maintains hair follicle stem cell homeostasis, thereby inhibiting premature hair loss; Technology transfer of cancer diagnostic biomarker technology to companies, etc.
### Kang-Gun Kim, International Exchange Committee Chairman

Research Fellow, Seoul National University Medical Research Institute; Postdoctoral Researcher, University of Oxford Medical School.
Graduate Student President, Oxford University Korean Student Association (OUKS); President, Korean Researchers in UK (KRUK); Co-Representative, Korean UK Life Scientists Association (KULSA); Chairman, Committee for World Korean Next Generation Leaders Convention (FLC), Overseas Koreans Agency, etc.
Established and operated academic, industrial, and medical networks; Facilitated collaborations with global industries such as AstraZeneca, Roche, and Google DeepMind, as well as major NHS hospitals and research teams from Oxford and Cambridge Universities.

sections.content: ### Related Articles: Chinese Central Media <Xinhua News Agency>
#### Xinhua News Agency
Source: Xinhua News Agency Korean News Service 2026-02-08 13:24:15
|Special Feature - Korea-China Exchange| Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA) Launched in Shanghai... Accelerating China-Korea Beauty Industry Cooperation

(Shanghai, China = Xinhua News Agency) The Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA) was officially launched in Shanghai, China on the 6th. The association plans to play a strategic role in promoting China-Korea skin health industry cooperation and support mutual market entry for companies from both countries.

The association will operate as a comprehensive cooperation platform with the participation of various stakeholders, including medical institutions, universities and research institutes, cosmetics, health food, and medical device companies, digital media sectors, and government agencies.
Group photo from the Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA) launching ceremony on the 6th.
#### Xinhua Finance
Xinhua Finance, Yang Zihua, Huang Anqi 02-06 22:55 Readership 475,000
Listen to the full text

South Korea's cosmetics exports have continued to grow in recent years, with China being the main export market. However, once popular K-beauty brands have shown significant differentiation in their performance in the domestic market in recent years, with some makeup brands like hince exiting, while others like Dr.G are firmly deepening their presence. The Korean government and business community are exploring organized industry chain collaboration and market resource integration to lower the entry barriers for small and medium-sized brands into China.

Xinhua Finance, Shanghai, February 6 (Yang Zihua, Huang Anqi) The Korea Skin Health Industry Association (KSHIA) was established in Shanghai on February 6. It is understood that the platform aims to provide systematic support for Korean companies entering the Chinese market and deepen China-Korea industrial cooperation.
Industry insiders point out that domestic consumers' demand for skin health management and high-quality products continues to rise, and the Chinese market shows strong appeal to the Korean industry.

sections.content: ## Linkage to 2026 Korea-China Industry-Academia-Research Large-scale Joint Project (Scheduled for July 2026)
Ministry of Science and ICT Announcement No. 2025-0737

The Ministry of Science and ICT invites applications for the 2025 'Korea-China Industry-Academia-Research Large-scale Joint Research Project' as follows, to promote mutual development through scientific and technological cooperation and strengthen scientific and technological competitiveness. We encourage interested researchers to apply.
<Supervising Ministry> Ministry of Science and ICT
<Managing Institution> National Research Foundation of Korea
### 1. Project Objectives

content: Strengthen the scientific and technological competitiveness of both countries by applying and creating industrial outcomes from basic science achievements through large-scale joint industry-academia-research projects with high demand from both countries.
Secure global leadership in the scientific and technological field through cooperation initiatives between the two countries and contribute to the realization of an innovative nation.

sections.content: ### 2. Supported Fields

content: ① BT (Biotechnology)
② ICT (Information and Communication Technology)
※ Applicants must consult with Chinese researchers and apply in the same field.

sections.content: ### 3. Support Period and Scale

content: □ Number of Selections: 2 projects
□ Support Scale: KRW 500 million per project per year
※ The support amount is the total funding including indirect costs, and indirect costs are classified as other expenses.
※ Chinese researchers will receive matching funds from the Ministry of Science and Technology of China.
□ Support Period: 3 years
□ Project Start: After November 2025

sections.content:
| Project Proposal Submission | Selection Evaluation | Project Selection | Project Selection Announcement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Principal Research Institution (Applicant) | Managing Institution | Selection in cooperation between both countries based on evaluation results (Ministry of Science and ICT, Korea / Ministry of Science and Technology, China) | Ministry of Science and ICT / National Research Foundation of Korea |
※ New projects are finally selected by agreement between the governments of Korea and China.
### 4. Selection Evaluation Procedure and Criteria

content: □ Evaluation Procedure
※ Presentation Evaluation
※ The presentation evaluation schedule will be announced separately after the application deadline.
□ Evaluation Criteria
o Necessity of Joint Research
o Appropriateness of Joint Research Goals and Implementation Plan
o Research Competence of Principal Investigator
o Appropriateness of Research Team Composition
o Potential for Development of Cooperation Network between Both Countries
o Expected Outcomes and Appropriateness of Commercialization Plan, etc.
※ The above evaluation criteria are subject to change according to the evaluation plan.

sections.content: ### 5. Project Structure

content: □ Project Structure: Composed of a principal project and two joint/consignment projects.
o Formation of a consortium centered around the principal research and development institution, with participation from universities, research institutes, and companies.
※ Formation of a consortium involving all three types of institutions (universities, research institutes, companies) is mandatory.
□ Principal Research and Development Institutions: Universities, national/public research institutes, government-funded research institutes, etc. (Korean side)
※ Institutions as specified in Article 2, Paragraph 3, Sub-paragraphs (a) through (f) of the 'National Research and Development Innovation Act'.

o Research Fund Allocation: The principal research and development institution (principal investigator) cannot allocate more than 50% of the government contribution to joint research institutions (co-principal investigators).
